summary: Tested WebSocket Chat and Reactions flow for ShowMeLive. All 8 backend tests passed. The WebSocket functionality is working correctly - viewers can send messages/reactions and creators receive them. The frontend Control Panel shows chat/reactions panel only when streaming is active (by design).
passed_tests: Backend: Viewer can connect to WebSocket
Backend: Creator can connect to WebSocket
Backend: Viewer sends message → Creator receives it
Backend: Viewer sends reaction → Creator receives it
Backend: Chat viewer count REST endpoint works
Backend: Events with chat_enabled=true are returned correctly
Backend: Events with reactions_enabled=true are returned correctly
Frontend: Control Panel loads with Go Live button
Frontend: Control Panel shows 'Chat connected!' toast when WebSocket connects
Frontend: Control Panel shows Live Chat and Live Reactions panels when streaming
Frontend: Event page shows Live Chat and Live Reactions badges
critical_code_review_comments: WebSocket implementation is correct - ChatConnectionManager properly broadcasts messages to all connected clients
Control Panel chat panel visibility is intentionally tied to isStreaming state (line 569 in ControlPanel.jsx)
LiveStreamViewer correctly connects to WebSocket when chat_enabled or reactions_enabled is true
seed_data_creation: Used existing test event: test-event-chat-1771457019709 with chat_enabled=true and reactions_enabled=true
context_for_next_testing_agent: WebSocket chat/reactions flow is fully functional. The Control Panel only shows chat/reactions panel when isStreaming=true (by design). Test event 'test-event-chat-1771457019709' has chat and reactions enabled and is in 'live' status.

websocket_chat.broadcast: Messages are broadcast to all connected clients including sender

control_panel_chat.websocket_connection: Connects when event loads with chat/reactions enabled

viewer_chat.connection_indicator: Shows '● Chat connected' or '○ Connecting to chat...'

websocket_flow_verified.viewer_to_creator_message: ✅ WORKING - Viewer sends message, creator receives it via WebSocket broadcast
websocket_flow_verified.viewer_to_creator_reaction: ✅ WORKING - Viewer sends reaction emoji, creator receives it via WebSocket broadcast
websocket_flow_verified.viewer_count_updates: ✅ WORKING - All clients receive viewer_count updates when connections change
websocket_flow_verified.connection_confirmation: ✅ WORKING - Both viewer and creator receive 'connected' message on WebSocket open
